key_insight: The paper improves the mixing-time guarantee of Dikin walks on polytopes from the previous $d^{2.5}$ bound to $d^{2.25}$ by using a scaled Lee--Sidford metric with stronger higher-order geometric control.
engineering_relevance: This strengthens the theoretical foundation for faster uniform/exponential sampling in high-dimensional constrained optimization and randomized algorithms that rely on polytope sampling.
method: Use a scaled Lee--Sidford metric instead of the previous Lewis-weight barrier.
Prove improved average self-concordance to raise Metropolis acceptance probability for random Dikin proposals.
Combine higher-order expansions, moving orthonormal frames, and Wiener-chaos decompositions to control Gaussian polynomial terms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a proven warm-start mixing-time improvement to $d^{2.25}$, plus an improved cold-start bound via annealing.
watch_item: The result is theoretical and depends on sophisticated higher-order analysis, so practical implementation may be sensitive to barrier computation and numerical stability.
paper_title: Beyond the $d^{2.5}$-mixing bound for Dikin walks on polytopes
